strands.name: Projector Implementation Progress
strands.theme: Building the tensor projector system for cognitive processing

claims.claim: A projector.py module has been created
A log_reader.py module is being developed to extract conversation turns from proxy logs
claims.grounding: Assistant turn 138 shows log_reader.py code written with Turn and ConversationTurn dataclasses

claims.claim: The system can parse both proxy logs (metadata-only) and session JSONL files (full content)
claims.grounding: log_reader.py contains read_proxy_log() and read_session_jsonl() functions with distinct purposes clearly documented

strands.name: Pichay Experiment Log Architecture
strands.theme: Understanding the log structure for the experiment

claims.claim: Proxy logs contain alternating request/response_stream entries in JSONL format
claims.grounding: log_reader.py docstring and read_proxy_log() implementation show this structure (turn 138)

claims.claim: Proxy logs include metadata (token counts, cache behavior, message sizes) but not full message content for privacy reasons
claims.grounding: read_proxy_log() docstring explains proxy logs have metadata but not full content by design; Turn dataclass contains token counts and cache metrics

claims.claim: Session JSONL files contain actual message content needed by the projector
claims.grounding: read_session_jsonl() function designed to extract actual user/assistant messages and tool interactions (turn 138)

claims.claim: Session JSONL format may vary with different shapes for content (string, list of blocks, etc.)
claims.grounding: read_session_jsonl() includes adaptive parsing for both simple strings and structured content lists with type checking (turn 138)

strands.name: Data Extraction Pipeline Design
strands.theme: How conversation data flows from logs into the projector

claims.claim: ConversationTurn dataclass captures turn_number, role, and content needed by projector
claims.grounding: ConversationTurn dataclass defined with these three fields (turn 138)

claims.claim: Tool calls and tool results are extracted and summarized in the content stream
claims.grounding: read_session_jsonl() includes blocks for tool_use and tool_result types, converting them to [tool_use: name] and [tool_result: ...] format (turn 138)

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Full system design and architecture of hamutay project
declared_losses.why: Only file creation confirmations and code snippets shown; no overview of how projector integrates into broader system provided
declared_losses.recovery_path: Next turn may provide architectural diagrams or system documentation

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Details of how projector output feeds into next reasoning cycles
declared_losses.why: Current content shows log_reader->projector pipeline start but not feedback loops or downstream consumption
declared_losses.recovery_path: Future conversation likely to reveal integration points when projector is used

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Actual sample log data and concrete examples
declared_losses.why: Code shows structure and parsing logic but no real proxy logs or session JSONLs shown
declared_losses.recovery_path: Testing phase will reveal real data shapes and edge cases

open_questions: How does the log_reader integrate with the projector module created in turn 136?
What is the complete data flow from raw logs through tensor projection to cognitive output?
How are Turn objects (metadata) and ConversationTurn objects (content) reconciled — are they cross-referenced?
What validation or error handling is applied when session JSONL format variations are encountered?
How will the projector handle turns where metadata exists (proxy log) but full content isn't available?
What is the entry point or main orchestration code that ties log_reader and projector together?
